title: Implement student registration and document upload backend in Motoko

requirements.text: Extend the backend Motoko actor to fully support student registration: ensure the Student type includes all fields from the Flask schema (full_name, email, mobile, course, category, income), and expose a registerStudent update call that inserts a new student record and returns the generated student ID.

quotes: INSERT INTO students (full_name, email, mobile, course, category, income)

acceptanceCriteria: Student type in main.mo includes: full_name, email, mobile, course, category, income fields
registerStudent update call accepts all six fields and stores the record
registerStudent returns the newly created student ID
Calling registerStudent twice with different data creates two distinct records

requirements.text: Extend the backend Motoko actor to support document upload tracking: ensure the DocumentRecord type includes student_id, document_name, file_path (stored as a Text blob/base64 or filename reference), and upload_status fields. Expose an uploadDocument update call that creates a new document record linked to a student and returns a success message.

quotes: INSERT INTO documents (student_id, document_name, file_path, upload_status)

acceptanceCriteria: DocumentRecord type includes: student_id, document_name, file_path, upload_status fields
uploadDocument update call accepts student_id, document_name, and file content (as Text/base64)
uploadDocument sets upload_status to 'Uploaded' on success
uploadDocument returns a success message confirming the document name

requirements.text: Update the frontend React Query hooks (useQueries.ts) to wire the registerStudent and uploadDocument backend calls to the new Motoko actor methods, replacing or supplementing any existing stubs so that the Profile page student creation and Documents page file upload both persist data through the ICP backend actor.
acceptanceCriteria: useRegisterStudent mutation calls the backend registerStudent actor method
useUploadDocument mutation calls the backend uploadDocument actor method with student_id, document_name, and file data
Profile page successfully creates a student record on form submit
Documents page successfully records an uploaded document linked to the current student
React Query cache is invalidated after successful mutations so lists refresh automatically

constraints: All backend logic must remain in a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo
Do not use Flask, SQLite, Python, or any external database — all persistence must use Motoko stable data structures
File uploads must be stored as base64 Text or filename references within the Motoko actor (no external file system)
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Migrating or running the Flask/SQLite backend — it is being replaced entirely by the Motoko actor
Implementing a real file storage system or CDN for uploaded documents
Adding new UI pages or redesigning existing pages
